title: Protocol for Assessing Lysozyme-Like Activity in Galleria mellonella Hemolymph using EnzChek Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hjxbj","text":"Bacterial or fungal infection in Galleria mellonella larvae produces an immune response in the host, affecting the antimicrobial enzymatic activity as the body tries to fight back the foreign elements causing the infection. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":33,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2ubl","text":"This is a protocol to evaluate lysozyme-like enzymatic activity in the cell-free hemolymph of Galleria mellonella larvae previously infected with a bacterial or fungal strain. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":94,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aes0r","text":"This is a fork from the previous protocol, modified to use the EnzChek Lysozyme Kit, for a more robust reading of lysozyme-like activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"701cn","text":"The anticoagulant buffer is taken from the described in DOI: 10.1159/000168009","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"blr2k","text":"Components of the EnzCheck Kit are light sensitive so prepare the reagents in the dark or a red lamp to avoid damaging the substrate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"wgtue","text":"Prepare anticoagulant buffer 93 mM NaCl, 100 mM glucose, 30 mM trisodium citrate, 26 mM citric acid, 10 mM Na2EDTA, and 0.1 mM phenylthiourea, pH 4.6","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":109,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"xmnng","text":"Ensure all equipment and reagents are sterile to prevent contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bpswl","text":"It is crucial to keep all samples on ice until they are processed to maintain enzyme activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3popo","text":"Anticoagulant Solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9ucmk","text":"93 mM NaCl, 100 mM glucose, 30 mM trisodium citrate, 26 mM citric acid, 10 mM Na2EDTA, and 0.1 mM phenylthiourea, pH 4.6. Filter-sterilized through a 0.45 nm pore filter","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d63av","text":"Sterile PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkh6t","text":"EnzCheck Lysozyme Assay Kit ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au3t1","text":"1.5 ml Eppendorf tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9qeai","text":"Crushed ice tray","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2b86u","text":"Liquid nitrogen for snap-freezing","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alg6a","text":"Refrigerated ultracentrifuge","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15bpd","text":"0.2 ml microtubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"abank","text":"Dark plastic 384 well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6rr7","text":"Fluorescence microplate reader","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Surface sterilize the blades of a pair of micro scissors or a scalpel by spraying them with 70% ethanol and allowing them to air dry.

Gently squeeze the larva to collect the dripping hemolymph (aim for 30-100 µl per larva) into a pre-chilled tube containing 30 µl of ice-cold anticoagulant buffer. Optional: use 100 µl pipette to collect the last fraction of hemolymph
Centrifuge the tubes at table top centrifuge at 12000 rpm for 1 minute to separate cellular debris from the hemolymph. Optional: If centrifuging at 4 °C, you can use 4000 x g for 10 minutes.
Cover the plate with a transparent lid or seal film, and then wrap in aluminum foil and incubate at 37 °C for 30 minutes.
Add 50 μl of 50 μg/ml DQ lysozyme substrate to all 8 tubes and mix.
Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader. Ex: 489 nm. Em: 523 nm
Optional: Measure every 5 minutes for 30 minutes total to get an average read value.
Dispense 30 µl of anticoagulant buffer into 0.2 ml microfuge tubes, and register their respective mass (1 tube per larva).
Take the overnight bacterial culture and transfer 2 ml into an Eppendorf tube
Grow 3 ml of an overnight bacteria culture at 37 °C in an incubator with vigorous shaking (150-200 rpm)
Centrifuge at no more that 1000 rcf, for 10 min at 4 °C.
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the bacterial pellet in 1 ml of sterile PBS
Measure the absorbance at 600 nm and dilute the suspension so it contains about 10^6 cells/ml
Select Galleria mellonela larvae in their 7th instar (usually, above 400 mg in weight) and distribute in Petri dishes as groups of 5 for each condition.
Prepare a Hamilton syringe with 10 µl of bacterial suspension or sterile PBS in case of control.
Grab a larva by its sides with the thumb and the index fingers and flip it upside down to expose its abdomen
Localize the hind/left proleg of the larva and carefully insert the needle of the syringe about 5 mm into the body of the larva
Inject the contents of the syringe slowly to prevent any leaking, and remove the syringe.
Return the larva to their respective dish and repeat for all the larvae and conditions.
Transfer the Petri dishes to an incubator and incubate the larvae for 0-6 hours at 37 °C
Optional: Anesthetize the larvae on ice for 5-10 minutes to minimize movement during hemolymph collection
Surface sterilize the larvae by spraying them with 70% ethanol over a paper towel and allow them to air dry.
Place the tube back on ice immediately after collection.
Repeat the collection steps for all the larvae.
With the blade, make a small cut in a abdomen of the larva.
Register the weight and add ice cold anticoagulant buffer to ensure the final ratio of hemolymph to buffer is 1:1
Optional: Carefully transfer aliquots of the clear supernatant (cell-free hemolymph) to a new pre-chilled tube.
Optional: Snap-freeze aliquots in liquid nitrogen and store at -20°C until ready for the next assay.
In a black plastic 384 well plate, add 30 μl of each mixture to three consecutive wells.
Generate the standard curve by averaging the fluorescence readings per concentration, and performing a linear regression.
Transfer the tubes to an ice tray and wait a couple of minutes so the contents of the tubes are ice-cold.
Prepare 8 microtubes with 50 μl of 1x reaction buffer
Optional: Flash freeze the tubes in liquid nitrogen and store at -20 °C until needed.
Add 50 μl of 1000 U/ml stock of lysozyme to the first tube. Mix well by pippetting
Prepare 1:2 serial dilutions with the next 6 tubes tube. The last tube should remain with reaction buffer only.
The following steps are done in the dark to avoid the formation of fluorescein conjugates.
All steps are done in the dark.
Pipette 15 μl of this mixture into three consecutive wells of a black plastic 384-well plate.
Mix 25 μl of CFH with 25 μl of 1x reaction buffer.
Add 15 μl of 50 μg/ml DQ lysozyme substrate to each well.
Cover the plate with a transparent lid or seal film, and then wrap in aluminum foil and incubate at 37 °C for 30 minutes.
Measure the fluorescence intensity with a top-reading microplate reader. Ex: 489 nm. Em: 523 nm
Optional: Measure every 5 minutes for 30 minutes total to get an average read value.
Pool the readings of each sample set and average the fluorescence intensity reading.
Use this value to calculate the lysozyme activity equivalent of the samples from the standard curve.
creator: Juan Jose Quispe Haro
